condition.name: Blinded
condition.description:
While you have the Blinded condition, you experience the following effects.

**Can't See.** You can't see and automatically fail any ability check that requires sight.

**Attacks Affected.** {action:Attack} rolls against you have {rule:Advantage}, and your attack rolls have {rule:Disadvantage}.

properties.text: You can't see and automatically fail any ability check that requires sight.
Attack rolls against you have advantage.

condition.name: Charmed
condition.description:
While you have the Charmed condition, you experience the following effects.

**Can't Harm the Charmer.** You can't attack the charmer or target the charmer with damaging abilities or magical effects.

**Social Advantage.** The charmer has {rule:Advantage} on any ability check to interact with you socially.

properties.text: You can't attack the charmer or target the charmer with damaging abilities or magical effects.
The charmer has advantage on any ability check to interact with you socially.

condition.name: Deafened
condition.description:
While you have the Deafened condition, you experience the following effect.

**Can't Hear.** You can't hear and automatically fail any ability check that requires hearing.

properties.text: You can't hear and automatically fail any ability check that requires hearing.

condition.name: Exhaustion
condition.description:
While you have the Exhaustion condition, you experience the following effects.

**Exhaustion Levels.** This condition is cumulative. Each time you receive it, you gain 1 Exhaustion level. You die if your Exhaustion level is 6.

**D20 Tests Affected.** When you make a {rule:D20 Test}, the roll is reduced by 2 times your Exhaustion level.

**Speed Reduced.** Your {rule:Speed} is reduced by a number of feet equal to 5 times your Exhaustion level.

**Removing Exhaustion Levels.** Finishing a {rule:Long Rest} removes 1 of your Exhaustion levels. When your Exhaustion level reaches 0, the condition ends.

properties.text: Death at Exhaustion level 6. Finishing a Long Rest removes 1 Exhaustion level.

condition.name: Frightened
condition.description:
While you have the Frightened condition, you experience the following effects.

**Ability Checks and Attacks Affected.** You have {rule:Disadvantage} on ability checks and attack rolls while the source of fear is within line of sight.

**Can't Approach.** You can't willingly move closer to the source of fear.

properties.condition: while source of fear is within line of sight
while source of fear is within line of sight

properties.text: You can't willingly move closer to the source of fear.

condition.name: Grappled
condition.description:
While you have the Grappled condition, you experience the following effects.

**Speed 0.** Your {rule:Speed} is 0 and can't increase.

**Attacks Affected.** You have {rule:Disadvantage} on attack rolls against any target other than the grappler.

**Movable.** The grappler can drag or carry you when it moves, but every foot of movement costs it 1 extra foot unless you are Tiny or two or more sizes smaller than it.

properties.condition: against any target other than the grappler

properties.text: The grappler can drag or carry you when it moves, but every foot of movement costs it 1 extra foot unless you are Tiny or two or more sizes smaller than it.

condition.name: Incapacitated
condition.description:
While you have the Incapacitated condition, you experience the following effects.

**Inactive.** You can't take any {rule:Action}, {rule:Bonus Action}, or {rule:Reaction}.

**No Concentration.** Your {status:Concentration} is broken.

**Speechless.** You can't speak.

**Surprised.** If you're Incapacitated when you roll {rule:Initiative}, you have {rule:Disadvantage} on the roll.

properties.text: You can't take any action, bonus action, or reaction.
Your Concentration is broken.
You can't speak.

condition.name: Invisible
condition.description:
While you have the Invisible condition, you experience the following effects.

**Surprise.** If you're Invisible when you roll {rule:Initiative}, you have {rule:Advantage} on the roll.

**Concealed.** You aren't affected by any effect that requires its target to be seen unless the effect's creator can somehow see you. Any equipment you are wearing or carrying is also concealed.

**Attacks Affected.** {action:Attack} rolls against you have {rule:Disadvantage}, and your attack rolls have {rule:Advantage}. If a creature can somehow see you, you don't gain this benefit against that creature.

properties.condition: if the target can't see you

properties.text: You aren't affected by any effect that requires its target to be seen unless the effect's creator can somehow see you. Any equipment you are wearing or carrying is also concealed.
Attack rolls against you have disadvantage if the attacker can't see you.

condition.name: Paralyzed
condition.description:
While you have the Paralyzed condition, you experience the following effects.

**Incapacitated.** You have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition.

**Speed 0.** Your {rule:Speed} is 0 and can't increase.

**Saving Throws Affected.** You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity {rule:Saving Throw|saving throws}.

**Attacks Affected.** {action:Attack} rolls against you have {rule:Advantage}.

**Automatic Critical Hits.** Any attack roll that hits you is a {rule:Critical Hit} if the attacker is within 5 feet of you.

properties.text: You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
Attack rolls against you have advantage.
Any attack roll that hits you is a critical hit if the attacker is within 5 feet of you.

condition.name: Petrified
condition.description:
While you have the Petrified condition, you experience the following effects.

**Turned to Inanimate Substance.** You are transformed, along with any nonmagical objects you are wearing and carrying, into a solid inanimate substance (usually stone). Your weight increases by a factor of ten, and you cease aging.

**Incapacitated.** You have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition.

**Speed 0.** Your {rule:Speed} is 0 and can't increase.

**Attacks Affected.** {action:Attack} rolls against you have {rule:Advantage}.

**Saving Throws Affected.** You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity {rule:Saving Throw|saving throws}.

**Resist Damage.** You have {rule:Resistance} to all damage.

**Poison Immunity.** You have {rule:Immunity} to the {condition:Poisoned} condition.

properties.text: You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
Attack rolls against you have advantage.
You are transformed into a solid inanimate substance. Your weight increases by a factor of ten and you cease aging.

condition.name: Poisoned
condition.description:
While you have the Poisoned condition, you experience the following effect.

**Ability Checks and Attacks Affected.** You have {rule:Disadvantage} on attack rolls and ability checks.

condition.name: Prone
condition.description:
While you have the Prone condition, you experience the following effects.

**Restricted Movement.** Your only movement options are to crawl or to spend an amount of movement equal to half your {rule:Speed} (round down) to right yourself and thereby end the condition. If your {rule:Speed} is 0, you can't right yourself.

**Attacks Affected.** You have {rule:Disadvantage} on attack rolls. An attack roll against you has {rule:Advantage} if the attacker is within 5 feet of you. Otherwise, that attack roll has {rule:Disadvantage}.

properties.text: Your only movement options are to crawl or to spend movement equal to half your Speed (round down) to stand up and end the condition. If your Speed is 0, you can't stand up.
Attack rolls against you have advantage if the attacker is within 5 feet, or disadvantage if the attacker is farther away.

condition.name: Restrained
condition.description:
While you have the Restrained condition, you experience the following effects.

**Speed 0.** Your {rule:Speed} is 0 and can't increase.

**Attacks Affected.** {action:Attack} rolls against you have {rule:Advantage}, and your attack rolls have {rule:Disadvantage}.

**Saving Throws Affected.** You have {rule:Disadvantage} on Dexterity {rule:Saving Throw|saving throws}.

properties.text: Attack rolls against you have advantage.

condition.name: Stunned
condition.description:
While you have the Stunned condition, you experience the following effects.

**Incapacitated.** You have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition.

**Saving Throws Affected.** You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity {rule:Saving Throw|saving throws}.

**Attacks Affected.** {action:Attack} rolls against you have {rule:Advantage}.

properties.text: You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
Attack rolls against you have advantage.

condition.name: Unconscious
condition.description:
While you have the Unconscious condition, you experience the following effects.

**Inert.** You have the {condition:Incapacitated} and {condition:Prone} conditions, and you drop whatever you're holding. When this condition ends, you remain {condition:Prone}.

**Speed 0.** Your {rule:Speed} is 0 and can't increase.

**Attacks Affected.** {action:Attack} rolls against you have {rule:Advantage}.

**Saving Throws Affected.** You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity {rule:Saving Throw|saving throws}.

**Automatic Critical Hits.** Any attack roll that hits you is a {rule:Critical Hit} if the attacker is within 5 feet of you.

**Unaware.** You're unaware of your surroundings.

properties.text: You drop whatever you're holding. When this condition ends, you remain Prone.
You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
Attack rolls against you have advantage.
Any attack roll that hits you is a critical hit if the attacker is within 5 feet of you.
You're unaware of your surroundings.

disease.name: Cackle Fever
disease.description:
Cheaply made potions and elixirs are sometimes tainted by Cackle Fever, which affects Humanoids only (gnomes are strangely immune). A creature suffers the following effects 1d4 days after infection.

**Fever.** The creature gains 1 {condition:Exhaustion} level, which lasts until the contagion ends on the creature.

**Uncontrollable Laughter.** While the creature has the {condition:Exhaustion} condition, the creature makes a DC 13 Constitution saving throw each time it takes damage other than Psychic damage. On a failed save, the creature takes 5 (1d10) Psychic damage and has the {condition:Incapacitated} condition as it laughs uncontrollably. At the end of each of its turns, the creature repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

**Fighting the Contagion.** At the end of each {rule:Long Rest}, an infected creature makes a DC 13 Constitution saving throw. After the creature succeeds on three of these saves, the contagion ends on it, and the creature is immune to Cackle Fever for 1 year.

**Spreading the Contagion.** Any Humanoid (other than a gnome) that starts its turn within a 10-foot Emanation originating from a creature infected with Cackle Fever must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or also become infected with the contagion. On a successful save, the Humanoid can't catch the contagion from that particular infected creature for the next 24 hours.

properties.text: Gains 1 Exhaustion level on infection (lasts until contagion ends). While Exhausted: on taking non-Psychic damage, DC 13 Constitution save or take 5 (1d10) Psychic damage and become Incapacitated with mad laughter (repeating save each turn to end, auto-success after 1 minute). Spreads via 10-foot Emanation (DC 10 Constitution save). Cured after three successful DC 13 Constitution saves at end of Long Rest; then immune for 1 year.

disease.name: Sewer Plague
disease.description:
Fouled potions and alchemical waste can give rise to Sewer Plague, which incubates in sewers and refuse heaps and is sometimes transmitted by creatures that dwell in such areas, including otyughs and rats. Any Humanoid that is wounded by a creature that carries the contagion or that comes into contact with contaminated filth or offal must succeed on a DC 11 Constitution saving throw or become infected with Sewer Plague. A creature suffers the following effects 1d4 days after infection.

**Fatigue.** The creature gains 1 {condition:Exhaustion} level.

**Weakness.** While the creature has any Exhaustion levels, it regains only half the normal number of Hit Points from spending Hit Point Dice.

**Restlessness.** While the creature has any Exhaustion levels, finishing a {rule:Long Rest} neither restores lost Hit Points nor reduces the creature's Exhaustion level.

**Fighting the Contagion.** Daily at dawn, an infected creature makes a DC 11 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature gains 1 {condition:Exhaustion} level as its fatigue worsens. On a successful save, the creature's Exhaustion level decreases by 1. If the creature's Exhaustion level is reduced to 0, the contagion ends on the creature.

properties.text: Gains 1 Exhaustion level on infection. While Exhausted: regains only half HP from spending Hit Dice, and Long Rest neither restores lost HP nor reduces Exhaustion level. Daily at dawn: DC 11 Constitution save — failed save gains 1 Exhaustion, successful save reduces Exhaustion by 1. Exhaustion reaching 0 ends the contagion.

disease.name: Sight Rot
disease.description:
Any Beast or Humanoid that drinks water tainted by Sight Rot must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or have the {condition:Blinded} condition until the contagion ends.

**Fighting the Contagion.** Magic such as a {spell:Heal} or {spell:Lesser Restoration} spell ends the contagion immediately. A character who is proficient with an Herbalism Kit can use it to create one dose of nonmagical ointment, which takes 1 hour. When applied to the eyes of a creature suffering from Sight Rot, the ointment suppresses the contagion on that creature for 24 hours. If the contagion is suppressed in this way for a total of 72 hours (requiring three doses and applications of the ointment), the contagion ends on the creature.

**Spreading the Contagion.** Any Humanoid that makes skin contact with a creature infected with Sight Rot must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or also become infected with the contagion. On a successful save, the Humanoid can't catch the contagion from that particular infected creature for the next 24 hours.

properties.text: Infected creature has the Blinded condition until the contagion ends. Cured by Heal or Lesser Restoration, or by three applications of Herbalism Kit ointment (1 hour each, 24 hours suppression per dose, 72 hours total). Spreads via skin contact (DC 15 Constitution save).

status.name: Bloodied
status.description: A creature is Bloodied while it has half its {rule:Hit Points} or fewer remaining.

status.name: Concentration
status.description:
Some spells and other effects require Concentration to remain active, as specified in their descriptions. If the effect's creator loses Concentration, the effect ends. If the effect has a maximum duration, the effect's description specifies how long the creator can concentrate on it: up to 1 minute, 1 hour, or some other duration. The creator can end Concentration at any time (no action required). The following factors break Concentration.

**Another Concentration Effect.** You lose Concentration on an effect the moment you start casting a spell that requires Concentration or activate another effect that requires Concentration.

**Damage.** If you take damage, you must succeed on a Constitution {rule:Saving Throw} to maintain Concentration. The DC equals 10 or half the damage taken (round down), whichever number is higher, up to a maximum DC of 30.

**Incapacitated or Dead.** Your Concentration ends if you have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition or you die.

properties.text: Concentration breaks when: (1) you start another Concentration effect, (2) you take damage and fail a Constitution save (DC = max(10, damage/2 rounded down), maximum DC 30), or (3) you gain the Incapacitated condition or die.

status.name: Surprised
status.description: If a creature is caught unawares by the start of combat, that creature is surprised, which causes it to have {rule:Disadvantage} on its {rule:Initiative} roll.
